title: App Stylesheet (App.css)
content:
This file contains the global CSS styles for the DocMed application. It defines color palettes, typography, layout structures, and styling for various components. ## Purpose - **Theming:** Establishes a consistent visual theme with predefined CSS variables for colors, spacing, and border-radius. - **Layout:** Defines the core layout structure, including the sidebar and main content area. - **Component Styling:** Provides base styles for navigation elements, search bars, markdown content, and other UI components. - **Responsiveness & Transitions:** Includes basic transitions for hover effects and sidebar collapsing. ## Key Styles and Variables ### CSS Variables - `--bg`: Background color (`#0b0d14`) - `--surface`: Surface color for cards and panels (`#111520`) - `--surface-2`: Slightly darker surface color (`#161b28`) - `--border`: Primary border color (`#1e2535`) - `--border-2`: Secondary border color (`#252f42`) - `--text`: Primary text color (`#e2e8f0`) - `--text-2`: Secondary text color (`#94a3b8`) - `--text-3`: Tertiary text color (`#475569`) - `--accent`: Accent color for interactive elements (`#22d3ee`) - `--accent-dim`: Dimmed accent color for backgrounds (`rgba(34, 211, 238, 0.12)`) - `--sidebar-w`: Sidebar width (`240px`) - `--radius`: Default border-radius (`8px`) ### Layout Classes - `.layout`: Main flex container for sidebar and main content. - `.sidebar`: Styles for the navigation sidebar. - `.sidebar.collapsed`: Styles when the sidebar is collapsed. - `.main`: Styles for the main content area. ### Component Styling Examples - **Navigation (`.nav`, `.nav-item`):** Styles for navigation links and groups, including active states and hover effects. - **Search (`.search-wrap`, `.search-input`):** Styles for the file search input and its associated icons. - **Markdown (`.markdown-body`):** Styles for rendering markdown content, including headings, paragraphs, links, code blocks, and tables. - **Cards (`.stat-card`, `.file-card`):** Consistent styling for various card elements used for statistics and file listings. ## Usage This stylesheet is typically imported into the main `App.js` or `index.js` file of the React application to apply global styles. Developers can reference these classes directly in their JSX components or create new components that utilize these base styles. **Example of applying a style:** ```jsx

Documentation Title

A brief description of the documentation.

{/* ... content ... */}
``` Developers can also extend or override these styles within their component-specific CSS files or by utilizing CSS-in-JS solutions.
